author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XI. 1.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding His twelve disciples, He departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, after He had sent them, He proceeded to withdraw Himself, to give them room and opportunity to do what He had enjoined.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while He was present and healing, no one would be willing to approach them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Jesus,he sent two ofhis disciples, and asked Him, saying, Art thou He that should come,or do we look for another?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Luke saith, they also told John of the miracles, and then he sent them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, this contains no matter of difficulty, but of consideration only; for this, among other things, indicates their jealousy towards Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what follows is completely among the controverted points.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of what nature then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Their saying, “Art Thou He that should come, or do we look for another?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, he that knew Him before His miracles, he that had learned it of the Spirit, he that heard it of the Father, he who had proclaimed Him before all men; doth he now send to learn of Him, whether it be Himself or no?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if yet thou didst not know that it is surely He, how thinkest thou thyself credible, affirming as thou dost concerning things, whereof thou art ignorant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that is to bear witness to others, must be first worthy of credit himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not say, “I am not meet to loose the latchet of His shoe?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not say, “I knew Him not, but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending and resting upon Him, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not see the Spirit in form of a dove? didst thou not hear the voice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not utterly forbid Him, saying, “I have need to be baptized of Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not say even to thy disciples, “He must increase, I must decrease?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not teach all the people, that “He should baptize them with the Holy Ghost and with fire?”and that He “is the Lamb of God that taketh away the sin of the world?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou not before His signs and miracles proclaim all these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then now, when He hath been made manifest to all, and the fame of Him hath gone out everywhere, and dead men have been raised, and devils driven away, and a display made of so great miracles, dost thou after this send to learn of Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the fact?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Were all these sayings a kind of fraud: a stage play and fables?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, who that hath any understanding would say so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say not, John, who leaped in the womb, who before his own birth proclaimed Him, the citizen of the wilderness, the exhibitor of the conversation of angels; but even though he were one of the common sort, and of them that are utterly outcast, he would not have hesitated, after so many testimonies, both on his own part and on the part of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is evident, that neither did he send as being himself in doubt, nor did he ask in ignorance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since no one surely could say this, that though he knew it fully, yet on account of his prison he was become rather timid: for neither was he looking to be delivered therefrom, nor if he did look for it, would he have betrayed his duty to God, armed as he was against various kinds of death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For unless he had been prepared for this, he would not have evinced so great courage towards a whole people, practised in shedding blood of prophets; nor would he have rebuked that savage tyrant with so much boldness in the midst of the city and the forum, severely chiding him, as though he were a little child, in hearing of all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And even if he were grown more timid, how was he not ashamed before his own disciples, in whose presence he had so often borne witness unto Him, but asked his question by them, which he should have done by others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet surely he knew full well, that they too were jealous of Christ, and desired to find some handle against Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how could he but be abashed before the Jewish people, in whose presence he had proclaimed such high things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or what advantage accrued to him thereby, towards deliverance from his bonds?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not for Christ’s sake had he been cast into prison, nor for having proclaimed His power, but for his own rebuke touching the unlawful marriage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what child so silly, what person so frantic, but that so he would have put on himself their character?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is it which he is bringing about?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that it belongs not to John to have doubt hereupon, no nor to any ordinary person, nor even to one extremely foolish and frenzied; so much is evident from what we have said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now we have only to add the solution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what intent then did he send to ask?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: John’s disciples were starting aside from Jesus, and this surely any one may see, and they had always a jealous feeling towards Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it is plain, from what they said to their master: “He that was with thee,” it is said, “beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all men come unto Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “There arose a question between John’s disciples and the Jews about purifying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again they came unto Him, and said, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but Thy disciples fast not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as yet they knew not who Christ was, but imagining Jesus to be a mere man, but John greater than after the manner of man, were vexed at seeing the former held in estimation, but the latter, as he had said, now ceasing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this hindered them from coming unto Him, their jealousy quite blocking up the access.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now so long as John was with them, he was exhorting them continually and instructing them, and not even so did he persuade them; but when he was now on the point of dying, he uses the more diligence: fearing as he did lest he might leave a foundation for bad doctrine, and they continue broken off from Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as he was diligent even at first to bring to Christ all that pertained to himself; so on his failing to persuade them, now towards his end he does but exert the more zeal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now if he had said, “Go ye away unto Him, He is better than I,” he would not have persuaded them, minded as they were not easily to be separated from him, but rather he would have been thought to say it out of modesty, and they would have been the more rivetted to him; or if he had held his peace, then again nothing was gained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then doth he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He waits to hear from them that Christ is working miracles, and not even so doth he admonish them, nor doth he send all, but some two (whom he perhaps knew to be more teachable than the rest); that the inquiry might be made without suspicion, in order that from His acts they might learn the difference between Jesus and himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he saith, Go ye, and say, “Art thou He that should come, or do we look for another?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Christ knowing the purpose of John, did not say, I am He; for this would again have offended the hearers, although this was what it naturally followed for Him to say, but He leaves them to learn it from His acts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it saith, “when these were come to Him, then “He cured many.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet what congruity was there, that being asked, “Art thou He,” He should say nothing to that, but should presently cure them that were sick; unless it had been His mind to establish this which I have mentioned?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because they of course would account the testimony of His deeds surer, and more above suspicion than that of His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing therefore, as being God, the mind with which John had sent them, He straightway cured blind, lame, and many others; not to teach him (for how should He him that was convinced), but these that were doubting: and having healed them, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Go and show John again those things which ye do hear and see; the blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, and the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, and the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he added, “And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me;”implying that He knows even their unuttered thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He had said, “I am He,” both this would have offended them, as I have already said; and they would have thought, even if they had not spoken, much as the Jews said to Him, “Thou bearest record of Thyself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith not this Himself, but leaves them to learn all from the miracles, freeing what He taught from suspicion, and making it plainer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He covertly added His reproof of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, because they were “offended in Him,” He by setting forth their case and leaving it to their own conscience alone, and by calling no witness of this His accusation, but only themselves that knew it all, did thus also draw them the more unto Himself, in saying, Blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed His secret meaning was of them when He said this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in order to our making the truth more evident to you by the comparison of the several statements, producing not only our own sayings, but also what is stated by others; we must needs add some account of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then do some affirm?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That this which we have stated was not the cause, but that John was in ignorance, yet not in ignorance of all; but that He was the Christ, he knew, but whether He was also to die for mankind, he knew not, therefore he said, “Art Thou He that should come?” that is, He that is to descend into hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this is not tenable; for neither of this was John ignorant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This at least he proclaimed even before all the others, and bare record of this first, “Behold,” saith he, “the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now he called Him a lamb, as proclaiming the cross, and again in saying, “That taketh away the sin of the world,” he declared this same thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not otherwise than by the cross did He effect this; as Paul likewise said: “And the handwriting which was contrary to us, even it He took out of the way, nailing it to His cross.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And his saying too, “He shall baptize you with the Spirit,”is that of one who was foretelling the events after the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well: that He was to rise again, he knew, say they, and that He was to give the Holy Ghost; but that He should likewise be crucified, he knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then was He to rise again, who had not suffered, nor been crucified?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how was this man greater than a prophet, who knew not even what the prophets knew?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that he was greater than a prophet, even Christ Himself bare record,but that the prophets knew of the passion is surely plain to every one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so Isaiah saith, “He is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearer is dumb.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And before this testimony also he saith, “There shall be a root of Jesse, and He that shall rise again to rule the Gentiles, in Him shall the Gentiles trust.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then speaking of His passion, and of the ensuing glory, he added, “And His rest shall be honor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this prophet foretold not only that He should be crucified, but also with whom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For,” saith he, “He was numbered with the transgressors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not this only, but that He should not even plead for Himself; “For this man,” he saith, “openeth not His mouth:” and that He should be unjustly condemned; “For in His humiliation,” saith he, “His judgment was taken away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And before this again, David both saith this, and describes the judgment hall.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Why,” saith he, “do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The kings of the earth stand up, and the rulers are gathered together against the Lord, and against His anointed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And elsewhere he mentions also the image of the cross, saying on this wise, “They pierced my hand and my feet,”and those things which the soldiers were emboldened to do, he adds with all exactness, “For they parted my garments,” saith he, “among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And elsewhere again he saith, that they also offered Him vinegar; “For they gave me,” saith He, “gall for my meat, and for my thirst they made me drink vinegar.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So then the prophets, so many years before, speak of the hall of judgment, and of the condemnation, and of them that were crucified with Him, and of the division of the garments, and of the lot cast upon them, and of many more things besides (for indeed it is unnecessary to allege all now, lest we make our discourse long): and was this man, greater than them all, ignorant of all these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, how should this be reasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why did he not say, “Art thou He that should come to hell,”but simply, “He that should come?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Although this were far more absurd than the others, I mean their saying, “he therefore said these things, that he might preach there also after his departure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To whom it were seasonable to say, “Brethren, be not children in understanding, howbeit in malice be ye children.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the present life indeed is the season for right conversation, but after death is judgment and punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For in hell,” it is said, “who will confess unto thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then were “the gates of brass burst, and the bars of iron broken in sunder”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By His body; for then first was a body shown, immortal, and destroying the tyranny of death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, this indicates the destruction of the might of death, not the loosing of the sins of those who had died before His coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if this were not so, but He have delivered all that were before Him from hell,how saith He, “It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this saying supposes that those are also to be punished; more mildly indeed, yet still that they are to be punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet they did also suffer here the most extreme punishment, nevertheless not even this will deliver them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if it is so with them, much more with such as have suffered nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What then?” one may say, “were they wronged, who lived before His coming?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, for men might then be saved, even though they had not confessed Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this was not required of them, but not to worship idols, and to know the true God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the Lord thy God,” it is said, “is one Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore the Maccabees were admired, because for the observance of the law they suffered what they did suffer; and the three children, and many others too amongst the Jews, having shown forth a very virtuous life, and having maintained the standard of this their knowledge, had nothing more required of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then it was sufficient for salvation, as I have said already, to know God only; but now it is so no more, but there is need also of the knowledge of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said, “If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin, but now they have no cloak for their sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So likewise with regard to the rule of practice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then murder was the destruction of him that committed it, but now even to be angry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then to commit adultery, and to lie with another man’s wife, brought punishment, but now even to look with unchaste eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as the knowledge, so also the rule of life is now made stricter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that there was no need of a forerunner there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, if unbelievers are after death to be saved on their believing, no man shall ever perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all will then repent and adore.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in proof that this is true, hear Paul saying, “Every tongue shall confess, and every knee shall bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, “The last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But there is no advantage in that submission, for it comes not of a rightly disposed choice, but of the necessity of things, as one may say, thenceforth taking place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then any more bring in such old wives’ doctrines, and Jewish fables.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear at least what Paul saith touching these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For as many as have sinned without law, shall also perish without law;”where his discourse is of those who lived in the time before the law; and, “As many as have sinned in the law, shall be judged by the law,”speaking of all after Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, “That the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness, and unrighteousness of men,”and, “indignation and wrath, tribulation and anguish upon every soul of man that worketh evil, of the Jew first, and also of the Gentile.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet countless were the evils which the Gentiles have suffered in this world, and this is declared alike by the histories of the heathens, and by the Scriptures that are in our hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who could recount the tragic calamities of the Babylonians, or those of the Egyptians?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in proof that they who, not having known Christ before His coming in the flesh, yet refrained from idolatry and worshipped God only, and showed forth an excellent life, shall enjoy all the blessings; hear what is said: “But glory, and honor, and peace to every one that worketh good, to the Jew first, and also to the Gentile.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that for their good deeds there are many rewards, and chastisements again, and penalties for such as have done the contrary?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where now, tell me, are the utter unbelievers in hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if those before Christ’s coming, who had not so much as heard the name of hell,nor of a resurrection, and were punished here, shall suffer punishment there also; how much more we that have been nurtured in so many lessons of strict virtue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how is it reasonable, asks one, that they that have never heard of hell,should fall into hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they will say, “If thou hadst threatened hell, we should have feared more, and have been sobered.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To be sure; (is it not so?) at our rate of living now, who hear daily the sayings about hell, and give no heed at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, there is this also to be said; that he who is not restrained by the judgments in sight, much less will he be restrained by those others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the less reasonable sort, and those of a grosser disposition, are wont to be sobered rather by things which are at hand, and straightway to happen, than by such as will come to pass a long time after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But over us,” one may say, “a greater fear is suspended, and herein were they wronged.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For first, there are not the same measuresset to us as to them, but much greater for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now they that have undertaken greater labors, ought to enjoy greater help.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it is no little help, that our fear has been increased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if we have an advantage over them in knowing things to come, they have an advantage over us in that the severe punishments are presently laid upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But there is something else, which the multitude say with respect to this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “where,” say they, “is God’s justice, when any one for sinning here, is punished both here and there?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would ye then I should put you in mind of your own sayings, that ye may no longer give us trouble, but furnish the solution from within yourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I have heard many of our people, if haply they were told of a murderer cut off in a court of justice, how they had indignation, and talked in this way: “This unholy and accursed wretch, having perpetrated thirty murders, or even many more, hath himself undergone one death only; and where is the justice of it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that ye yourselves confess, that one death is not sufficient for punishment; how give ye then an opposite sentence now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because not others but yourselves are the objects of your judgment: so great a hindrance is self-love to our perceiving what is just.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because of this, when we are judging others, we search out all things with strictness, but when we are sitting in judgment on ourselves, we are blinded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if we were to search into these things in our own case too, as we do with regard to other men, we should give an uncorrupt sentence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we also have sins, deserving not two or three, but ten thousand deaths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to pass over all the rest, let us recollect ourselves, as many of us as partake unworthily of the mysteries; such men being guilty of the body and blood of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, when thou art talking of the murderer, take account of thyself also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he indeed hath murdered a man, but thou art under the guilt of slaying the Lord; and he, not having partaken of mysteries, but we, while enjoying the benefit of the sacred table.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what are they that bite and devour their brethren, and pour out such abundance of venom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is he that robs the poor of their food?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he who imparts not of his own, is such as I have said, much more he that takes the things of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How many robbers do the covetous surpass in wickedness! how many murderers and robbers of tombs, the rapacious! and how many after spoiling men are desirous even of their blood!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Nay,” saith he, “God forbid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now thou sayest, God forbid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When thou hast an enemy, then say, God forbid, and call to mind what hath been said, and show forth a life full of great strictness; lest the portion of Sodom await us also, lest we suffer the lot of Gomorrha, lest we undergo the ills of the Tyrians and Sidonians; or rather, lest we offend Christ, which were a thing more grievous and more to be feared than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though to many hellseem to be a fearful thing, yet I for my part will not cease continually to say, that this is more grievous and fearful than any hell; and you I entreat to be of the same mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so shall we both be delivered from hell, and enjoy the glory that is bestowed of Christ; unto which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
